core_payload.content:
# Agent Instruction Surfaces

## 1. Role
Every serious N1Hub agent will enter the repository through a durable local instruction stack before any large tool loop begins. Repository law, doctrine summaries, indexes, and machine-readable exports will remain always-present operating surfaces instead of optional prompt cargo.

## 2. Future Instruction Stack
- `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `WORKFLOW.md` acting as an explicit layered instruction surface
- machine-readable exports such as full-vault and codebase index packs
- concise always-on surfaces that still preserve broad project literacy
- lane-declared load order so every major swarm lane states which instruction surfaces govern it
- control-plane awareness of which instruction stack applies to each assignment family

## 3. Operational Consequence
Agents will enter N1Hub already oriented to repository law, project structure, capsule doctrine, and blast-radius constraints. Skills will remain procedural, while instruction surfaces will carry broad repository and framework truth.

## 4. Philosophical Alignment
Agent Instruction Surfaces supports To Dig Deep by making versioned local doctrine the default onboarding path for every serious agent lane.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Agent Instruction Surfaces defines the future durable instruction stack for N1Hub agents: repo doctrine, compact indexes, machine-readable exports, lane-declared load order, and explicit governance over which surfaces guide each assignment family.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Stripped meta-commentary leakage. Dream now asserts future state directly.
